message:
feat(mvcc,journal,persist,fuse): version chain backpressure, JBD2 writer, persist layer, FUSE write ops

Why these changes exist:

With the core engine now capable of journaled writes (previous commit),
the supporting layers — MVCC concurrency control, the JBD2 journal, the
persistence abstraction, and the FUSE interface — all needed corresponding
expansions to complete the read-write story end to end.

MVCC store (ffs-mvcc, ~1900 lines):
- Adds chain-length backpressure: when a block's version chain exceeds a
  configurable cap, the store raises CommitError::ChainBackpressure instead
  of silently growing unbounded.  A critical-length threshold triggers
  force-advancement of the oldest active snapshot to reclaim versions,
  preventing OOM under sustained write workloads.
- Introduces crossbeam-epoch (EBR) based deferred version reclamation:
  retired versions are linked into the epoch collector and physically freed
  only after all concurrent readers have exited.  EbrVersionStats and
  ebr_stats()/ebr_collect() expose reclamation counters for monitoring.
- BlockVersionStats reports per-store metrics (tracked blocks, max chain
  length, chains exceeding cap/critical thresholds) for operational
  dashboards.
- Transaction::record_read() now deduplicates: only the first read of a
  given block is recorded (the version seen at snapshot time), reducing
  read-set memory for transactions that re-read the same blocks.
- Re-exports CompressionPolicy as a public API for downstream crate use.
- Adds trace-level structured logging (ffs::ssi target) for read-set
  additions to aid SSI conflict debugging.

Persist layer (ffs-mvcc/persist.rs, ~255 lines):
- New module providing durable snapshotting of MVCC store state to SQLite
  (or pluggable backends).  Captures version chains, watermarks, and
  commit sequences so that a crashed process can restore MVCC state without
  full journal replay.

WAL benchmark (ffs-mvcc/benches/wal_throughput.rs, ~675 lines):
- Major expansion of the WAL throughput benchmark to cover the new
  backpressure and EBR reclamation paths.  Adds scenarios for chain-cap
  enforcement under concurrent writers, snapshot force-advance latency,
  and EBR collect() throughput, giving CI a regression signal for the
  write-path hot loop.

JBD2 journal writer (ffs-journal, ~635 lines):
- Implements Jbd2Writer — the write-side counterpart to the existing
  replay_jbd2 reader.  Produces JBD2-compatible descriptor, data, revoke,
  and commit blocks in a journal region.  Self-replayability is verified:
  a written journal can be immediately replayed by the existing replay_jbd2
  and yields identical block contents.
- Jbd2Transaction provides a builder API: add_write(target, payload) and
  add_revoke(target) stage operations; commit() serializes them into the
  journal region with proper header magic, sequence numbers, and block tags.
- Jbd2Writer::open() scans an existing journal region to discover the tail
  position and next sequence number, enabling append-after-recovery.
- Jbd2WriteStats captures descriptor/data/revoke/commit block counts for
  each committed transaction.
- Adds crc32c dependency for commit block checksums.

FUSE layer (ffs-fuse, ~290 lines):
- Implements the write-side Filesystem trait methods: setattr, write,
  create, mkdir, unlink, rmdir, rename, symlink, link, and fsync.
- Each method checks a read_only flag (propagated from mount options) and
  returns EROFS if the mount is read-only, preserving backward
  compatibility with the existing read-only FUSE mode.
- Adds reply helper functions (reply_error_empty, reply_error_write,
  reply_error_create) for the new reply types.
- The --rw flag in FrankenFuse options controls whether write operations
  are enabled, defaulting to read-only.
- Imports SetAttrRequest from ffs-core to translate FUSE's partial setattr
  fields into the structured request the core engine expects.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
